question:
Why is "code-switching" — adjusting your language and behavior depending on the social setting — often described as an unfair burden placed on marginalized groups?
A) It is a medically exhausting condition with no social dimension.
B) It requires marginalized individuals to constantly suppress their authentic identity to meet the comfort standards of a dominant culture.
C) Only politicians are required to code-switch, so it is a professional hazard, not a social one.
D) Code-switching improves cognitive function and is therefore always beneficial.
